title: GTM Engineering Intern
descriptionHtml:
Astronomer empowers data teams to bring mission-critical software, analytics, and AI to life and is the company behind Astro, the industry-leading unified DataOps platform powered by Apache Airflow®. Astro accelerates building reliable data products that unlock insights, unleash AI value, and powers data-driven applications. Trusted by more than 800 of the world's leading enterprises, Astronomer lets businesses do more with their data. To learn more, visit www.astronomer.io.

About this role:

Come join us for a paid, high-impact summer 2026 internship in NYC!

Ready to build the systems that drive a high-growth company? You'll sit at the absolute epicenter of our business - the intersection of Engineering and all our go-to-market teams (Sales, Marketing, Customer Success). We're looking for someone to build AI-powered workflows and high-leverage tools that turn big ideas into working, revenue-driving systems. It’s a chance to take full ownership, ship real code, and experiment with the latest AI tech.

What you get to do:

  • Ship internal tools and AI-powered workflows that support Sales and Marketing. Examples could include automated prospect research, outbound email generation, and lead scoring

  • Partner with GTM stakeholders and leadership to identify inefficiencies and automate repetitive workflows

  • Build prototypes using LLMs, embeddings, and retrieval techniques to enhance go-to-market execution

  • Turn high-impact manual processes into scalable systems

  • Test new product features and provide structured feedback to engineering

  • Contribute production-quality code in Go, Python, or React/TypeScript

  • Explore novel applications of AI within data orchestration and revenue operations

What you bring to the role:

  • Currently pursuing a Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field

  • Comfortable writing production-quality code in at least one language (Python, Go, JavaScript, TypeScript, etc.)

  • Interested in the intersection of AI, automation, and business impact

  • Excited about building tools that drive measurable outcomes

  • Strong problem-solving skills and comfort working with ambiguity

  • Excellent communicator who can collaborate cross-functionally

Bonus points if you have:

  • Built or experimented with AI agents or automation workflows

  • Experience working with LLM APIs (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.)

  • Built internal tools or revenue-supporting systems

  • Contributed to open-source projects

  • Experience building prototypes in fast-moving environments

  • Familiarity with Apache Airflow or data engineering concepts

The hourly pay range for this position is $30–$40 per hour. Actual compensation will be determined based on skills, experience, and qualifications.

At Astronomer, we value diversity. We are an equal opportunity employer: we do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status.
